home.title: When the planet gets sick, to regenerate is to heal and celebrate life
home.lead: Faced with the global challenges of climate change, pollution, and biodiversity loss, we offer local and practical solutions that regenerate the ecosystems sustaining life for present and future generations.
home.what_p1: Many individuals and organizations wish to live and act in a sustainable and regenerative way, but they do not always know how to do so or how to measure their impact.
home.what_p2: Every transformation process is unique and requires clarity, structure, and continuous learning.
home.what_p3: LivLin exists to accompany this process through strategic design, regenerative infrastructure, and monitoring and evaluation systems.
home.what_p4: Our purpose is to strengthen the health of ecosystems and organizations in order to build truly vibrant presents and futures.

services.title: Professional Services for Regenerative Living
services.lead: We organize our work into three service areas:
services.lead_emotional: Every action—individual or collective—no matter how simple it may seem, has an environmental impact due to its intended and unintended effects on the broader natural ecosystem that sustains life as a whole.

s1.title: Regenerative Ecosystem Design
s1.subtitle: Unlock the potential of the ecosystem you inhabit
s1.desc: We support the expression of nature in the city, from small terrace and backyard gardens to plazas and larger plots, cultivating environmental education and reconnection with the rhythms of nature. We promote agroecology-based urban agriculture, free of agrochemicals, regenerative, and adapted to each environment.
s1.li1: Personalized technical support throughout all stages of urban agroecological cultivation:
s1.sub_li3_6: Support in sowing, management, and harvesting
s1.sub_li3_7: Everything you need for your garden

s2.title: Organizational Transformation
s2.subtitle: Organizational Consulting and Environmental Education. Support processes and educational experiences for regenerative change
s2.desc: We accompany processes of deep change in individuals and organizations, strengthening regenerative capacities that sustain the conditions necessary to ensure the continuity of healthy and biodiverse life.
s2.li1: Process facilitation and strategic planning based on the Theory of Change model
s2.li1_desc: Theory of Change refers to a planning process that articulates how to achieve the desired change, including the definition of indicators and monitoring and evaluation plans for learning. This applies to individuals and organizations wishing to engage in the processes needed to define their path forward and achieve meaningful change.
s2.li2: Combination of advanced social science research methods for regenerative purposes
s2.li2_desc: Science to gather evidence, learn, and improve strategic decisions that connect with broader local and global conceptual frameworks, enabling participation in aligned local and international networks.
s2.sub_li2_2_desc: Interviews, group interviews, focus groups, mapping
s2.sub_li2_3_desc: Promotion of participatory leadership through methodologies such as World Café, talking circles, open space, wise action design, among others.
s2.li3: Workshop facilitation and training, individual and group mentoring
s2.li3_desc: Topics cover diverse local practices and actions that positively contribute to a regenerative and sustainable future vision. Content addresses environmental education themes through experiential learning for all ages, using a participatory leadership approach.
s2.li4: Advisory support in applying for and implementing public and private grant-funded projects
s2.li4_desc: Applicable in both national and international contexts

s3.title: Climate Action Monitoring and Evaluation
s3.subtitle: Appropriate technologies for climate action monitoring, evaluation, and learning
s3.desc: By creating web applications, we unlock practical processes that help organizations working for regeneration move forward more fluidly. We do this by developing low-cost, customized digital tools that support management, communication, and learning for aligned groups, always within a critical reflection on the use of technology for regenerative purposes.
s3.li1: Creation of websites for your organization.
s3.li1_desc: Simple, low-cost websites to communicate the value of your project and/or organization.
s3.li2: Creation of web applications tailored to your needs and interests:
s3.li2_desc: We co-create a digital solution for various organizational challenges and regenerative work.
s3.sub_li2_1: Financial management and savings systems for organizations
s3.sub_li2_3: Dynamic monitoring dashboards to explore data, manage information, and communicate results
s3.sub_li2_4: Territorial, social, or environmental data explorers
s3.sub_li2_5: Indicator monitoring dashboards with exportable visualizations
s3.sub_li2_7: Custom-designed solutions tailored to your needs

contact.title: Contact
contact.lead: Connect through any of these channels
contact.note: Thank you for your interest. We will reply soon.

propuesta_valor.title: Value Proposition
propuesta_valor.desc: All professional services offered are sustained by a present and future vision where:
propuesta_valor.li1: The global challenges of climate change, biodiversity loss, and the pollution crisis are faced collectively by people, organizations, communities, and companies committed to life.
propuesta_valor.li2: The act of regenerating the conditions for the continuity of life is central to guiding efforts toward a better way of living.
propuesta_valor.li3: We are willing to embrace a mindset shift that breaks away from linear and degenerative life patterns that result in greater pollution and biodiversity loss.
propuesta_valor.li4: We adopt new roles by integrating natural and human systems, in favor of the health of both.
propuesta_valor.li5: We assume that this work is in permanent evolution, as the regenerative perspective involves achieving goals whose timescale surpasses our human lifetimes; that is, objectives that transcend into future generations.

publico_interes.title: Who We Primarily Serve
publico_interes.li1: Anyone interested in joining the pulse of regeneration and seeking guidance.
publico_interes.li2: Individuals and communities interested in learning to grow their own food and starting a garden.
publico_interes.li3: People experiencing eco-anxiety who need help taking concrete actions to improve their health and that of the ecosystem.
publico_interes.li4: Professionals and technicians creating social impact who need support to ensure their work also contributes positively to the health of the greater ecosystem.
publico_interes.li5: Organizations and companies seeking to strengthen their sustainability practices, generating an impact that benefits both their well-being and that of the ecosystems where they live and envision their future.
publico_interes.li6: Educational institutions that need to strengthen their approach to environmental education through concrete regenerative actions within their spaces, enriching the meaning of their educational projects.
